page: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. This x86_64 architecture CPU features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16), AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance across multiple domains. In stress-ng and Geekbench tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance rank in the top 25%. The server demonstrates high memory bandwidth for small-block cached reads and large-block writes, though large-block uncached reads fall into the average tier. Cryptographic and mathematical operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by top 25% scores in Passmark encryption and physics tests. For database workloads, the instance achieves 67.43 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and ranks in the top 25% for Passmark database operations. Additionally, it shows top 10% performance for small-model (135M) LLM inference, while medium (7B) and large (70B) model inference speeds range from average to strong.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7a.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density with its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-speed DDR5 memory. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable compute power. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means storage-heavy applications must rely on network-attached storage, which may impact overall I/O latency. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, medium-scale web serving, and small-to-medium language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and 128.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 system delivers strong CPU and memory performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark benchmarks. It features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and SHA_NI, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is established at a 12 Gbps baseline. The server excels in database operations, achieving 67.43 million operations per second in Redis SET tests, and demonstrates top-tier efficiency in small-model LLM inference. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the m7a.8xlarge is suited for software compilation, database hosting, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 12 Gbps network baseline, delivering strong database and compute performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated AMD-powered general-purpose server offering high-speed DDR5 memory and strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (4.0 GB per core)
Strong top-25% performance in multi-core CPU and database benchmarks
Top-10% execution speeds for small-model LLM inference tasks
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
